findings.evidence: `rg -n "stdComplexLine|\[expose\]" code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/JHolomorphicLine.lean` shows `@[expose]` at lines 52 and 57, while the intended API is separately provided by `stdComplexLineReal_fst`, `stdComplexLineImag_snd`, `product_apply_stdComplexLineReal`, and `stdComplexLine_eq_smul_real_add_smul_imag` on lines 62-90.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from both definitions and add explicit characteristic restatements if needed, e.g. named `*_def` or sufficient `[simp]` coordinate lemmas, so consumers do not depend on reducibility.
findings.issue: `stdComplexLineReal` and `stdComplexLineImag` are marked `@[expose]`, making their bodies part of the public API even though the file already provides coordinate/product/decomposition lemmas for use without unfolding.

summary: The new standard-line API exposes implementation bodies for the coordinate-vector definitions instead of providing the needed characteristic theorem surface.
